pattern: Binary search directly on the answer value: for any candidate product P, count how many pairs multiply to at most P using per-element binary searches that respect each factor's sign, then binary-search that monotonic count for k.
hints: Don't generate every product -- binary search over the range of possible product values and ask 'how many pairs give a product <= this value?' for each candidate.

For a fixed element from nums1, because nums2 is sorted, a single binary search on nums2 tells you how many partners keep the product under your threshold -- but whether you search for an upper or lower bound depends on the sign of that nums1 element.

Binary search the smallest threshold P where count(products <= P) >= k; handle positive, negative, and zero multipliers from nums1 as three separate counting cases, using floor/ceiling division carefully for negatives.

edge_inputs.description: extreme negative and positive values at the constraint limits
mixed signs requiring correct floor/ceiling handling of negative division
k is the very last (largest) product
a zero sits among negative and positive values, affecting the count boundary
differently sized arrays with several negatives

stress.description: random sorted arrays with random k, exercising the binary search at realistic scale
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n1 = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    n2 = max(1, min(size, 20000))
    nums1 = sorted(rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n1))
    nums2 = sorted(rng.randint(-100000, 100000) for _ in range(n2))
    k = rng.randint(1, n1 * n2)
    return [nums1, nums2, k]
